data.title: Oracle Japan (TSE:4716) Net Margin Softness Tests Bullish Earnings Narratives
data.description:
Advertisement

Oracle Corporation Japan (TSE:4716) Q3 2026 Earnings Snapshot

Oracle Corporation Japan (TSE:4716) has put another solid quarter on the board, with recent fiscal period revenue running between ¥61,380 million and ¥70,618 million and basic EPS ranging from about ¥108.96 to ¥123.96, backed by trailing twelve month EPS of ¥478.63 and net income of ¥61,296 million. Over the past six reported quarters, the company has seen revenue move from ¥61,380 million in Q2 2025 to ¥68,402 million in Q2 2026, while basic EPS has ranged from ¥108.96 to ¥123.96 across that stretch, giving you a clear read on the earnings power behind the latest Q3 2026 update. With a trailing net margin of 22.5% and forecasts pointing to revenue growth ahead of the broader Japan market, the current results frame a business where profitability and top line momentum are central to the story.

See our full analysis for Oracle Corporation Japan.

With the recent numbers on the table, the next step is to see how this earnings profile lines up against the main narratives around Oracle Corporation Japan, and where those stories might need a refresh.

See what the community is saying about Oracle Corporation Japan

Revenue trend stays above market growth

  • On a trailing basis, revenue is ¥272,892 million, and forecasts point to about 8.5% annual growth compared with 5.4% for the broader Japan market.
  • Consensus narrative expects cloud partnerships and client migrations to support that revenue pace, while the recent shift from ¥61,380 million in Q2 2025 to ¥68,402 million in Q2 2026 shows the top line moving in line with what bulls see as sustained demand for Oracle’s cloud services.
    • Supporters of the bullish view highlight long term revenue growth assumptions of around 8.6% a year and reference customers across sectors using Oracle Cloud, which fits with the steady move in quarterly revenue shown in the data.
    • At the same time, bears point to scenarios with only 6.1% revenue growth, and the fact that on premise licenses are expected to stabilize or soften means investors can cross check those lower growth cases against the current ¥272,892 million trailing revenue base.

Analysts who see more upside argue this revenue profile could support higher long term earnings, while skeptics focus on slower growth cases, so it can be useful to see how those bullish assumptions stack up against the detailed narrative for Oracle Corporation Japan 🐂 Oracle Corporation Japan Bull Case

Margins steady, but a touch softer

  • Net income over the last twelve months is ¥61,296 million on revenue of ¥272,892 million, which works out to a 22.5% net margin compared with 23.1% a year earlier and five year earnings growth of about 4.9% a year.
  • Bears argue that a move from 23.1% to 22.5% net margin, combined with rising personnel and restructuring costs, supports a cautious view that future earnings could be pressured if cloud growth does not fully make up for expected license declines.
    • The bearish narrative explicitly builds in margin outcomes between 22.2% and 22.9%, so the current 22.5% sits right inside that range and does not yet contradict the concern that additional talent and AI investments might cap margin expansion.
    • At the same time, the trailing 5.2% earnings growth over the past year on this margin base challenges the idea that profitability is weakening sharply, because earnings are still edging up even with that small margin dip.

Cautious investors watch this small margin move closely and may want to see how the full bearish narrative reconciles cost pressures with cloud growth assumptions before forming a view on how durable current earnings are 🐻 Oracle Corporation Japan Bear Case

Valuation sits between DCF and targets

  • At a share price of ¥8,496, the stock is trading below a DCF fair value of ¥12,658.00 and also below the analyst price target of ¥15,271.43, while the trailing P/E of 17.7x sits slightly above the peer average of 17.1x and the Japan software industry average of 16.9x.
  • Consensus narrative notes that analysts as a group see earnings growing to ¥79.6 billion by 2029 and anticipate a higher future P/E of 29.7x, yet the current P/E premium to peers is modest and sits alongside an assessment that the shares are about 32.9% below the DCF fair value, which gives investors two very different valuation anchors to weigh.
    • What stands out is that the same data set can support a conservative multiple based on today’s 17.7x and a much higher implied multiple in the consensus scenario, so readers can stress test how comfortable they are with that gap.
    • At the same time, earnings growth over the last five years of roughly 4.9% a year is far below the growth rates implied in the long term scenarios, which makes it useful to compare those future expectations with the company’s actual track record when thinking about valuation.

data.title: Andean Precious Metals Q3 EPS Surge Reinforces Bullish Narrative On Earnings Power
data.description:
Andean Precious Metals (TSX:APM) has reported third quarter FY 2025 revenue of US$90.4 million and basic EPS of US$0.29, with earnings over the past five years growing 16.9% per year and 31.3% in the last year. Over recent periods, total revenue has moved from US$68.3 million in Q3 2024 to US$73.7 million in Q2 2025 and US$90.4 million in Q3 2025. Trailing twelve month basic EPS has progressed from US$0.13 in Q4 2024 to US$0.52 by Q3 2025. Together, these figures present a picture of solid profitability and margins that investors will weigh against the quality and sustainability of these results.

See our full analysis for Andean Precious Metals.

With the headline numbers on the table, the next step is to see how this earnings profile lines up against the prevailing narratives around Andean Precious Metals and where those stories might be reinforced or challenged.

See what the community is saying about Andean Precious Metals

Advertisement

Margins stay firm at 25.9% despite one off boost

  • Trailing net profit margin sits at 25.9%, only slightly above last year’s 25.6%, even though the last 12 months include a sizeable one off gain of US$31.7 million.
  • Bears argue that future profitability is at risk. However, this margin stability contrasts with their concern that operational issues at Golden Queen and dependence on Bolivian ore sourcing will quickly pressure net margins, especially given San Bartolomé’s current gross margin ratio of 43.8% and cash gross operating margin of US$16.13 per silver equivalent ounce.
    • The bearish narrative points to heap leach execution risk and potential higher costs. The reported 25.9% margin shows that, for now, those issues have not driven margins meaningfully below last year’s 25.6% level.
    • Critics also highlight reliance on community and third party ore agreements. Current margins suggest that, up to this point, those arrangements have supported mill utilization rather than dragging overall profitability lower.
Bears warn that a comfortable margin today could mask operational headaches at Golden Queen and San Bartolomé. It can be useful to see how that argument is laid out in full before deciding how much weight to give it.🐻 Andean Precious Metals Bear Case

Record US$43.7m quarterly profit meets mixed growth stories

  • Net income excluding extra items reached US$43.7 million in Q3 2025 on US$90.4 million of revenue, compared with US$17.4 million in Q2 2025 and US$14.6 million in Q1 2025, contributing to trailing 12 month net income of US$77.5 million.
  • Bullish investors point to this strong earnings base and record quarter as a platform for future moves. The consensus narrative expects revenue to grow 13.6% per year and earnings to reach US$94.5 million by about 2029. This contrasts with the separate forecast set that calls for a 13.3% annual revenue decline and a 35.3% annual earnings decline over the next three years.
    • The 5 year earnings growth rate of 16.9% per year and last year’s 31.3% earnings growth support the idea that the business has produced solid profits recently. This lines up more closely with the bullish and consensus expectations than with steep near term decline forecasts.
    • At the same time, the presence of a US$31.7 million one off gain in the trailing period is a concrete reason why some analysts are more cautious about extrapolating the current US$77.5 million earnings run rate too far into the future.

P/E of 9.1x versus targets and DCF fair value

  • The shares trade on a P/E of 9.1x against peers at 27.9x and the Canadian Metals & Mining industry at 16.4x. The current share price of CA$6.50 sits well below the analyst price target of CA$15.05 and the DCF fair value of CA$90.41.
  • Bulls see this valuation gap as an opportunity, arguing that strong trailing earnings and margins justify a higher multiple. The bearish narrative questions whether forecasts for declining earnings of about 35.3% per year and revenue declining 13.3% per year over the next three years will keep the market from closing that gap quickly.
    • Analyst targets implying large upside and a DCF fair value of CA$90.41 rely on the company continuing to generate healthy profits. The forecasted earnings decline highlights why some investors may hesitate to re rate the stock closer to peer or industry P/Es.
    • What stands out is the tension between a low 9.1x P/E on a trailing margin of 25.9% and the expectation that those earnings could fall meaningfully. This helps explain the wide range of views embedded in current targets around CA$15.05.
Supporters of the bullish view often point to this combination of a low 9.1x P/E and strong recent margins. Getting the full bullish narrative can help you judge whether that valuation gap to their thesis still looks appealing on your own terms.🐂 Andean Precious Metals Bull Case

data.title: Is Kratos (KTOS) Using Its 5G NTN Win To Recast Its Defense-Centric Technology Narrative?
data.description:
• Kratos Defense & Security Solutions recently announced it was selected by SKY Perfect JSAT to develop and validate the 5G Non-Terrestrial Network ground system for the Universal NTN initiative in Asia-Pacific, using its software-based OpenSpace platform to support standards-aligned, interoperable satellite-to-5G connectivity.
  • This contract highlights Kratos’ role in bringing satellites into mainstream mobile networks, potentially broadening its relevance beyond traditional defense into commercial communications infrastructure.
  • Next, we’ll explore how this 5G NTN ground-system win with SKY Perfect JSAT could reshape Kratos’ investment narrative around multi-domain technologies.

Kratos Defense & Security Solutions Investment Narrative Recap

To own Kratos, you have to believe its mix of defense unmanned systems and software-based space infrastructure can justify today’s premium valuation and modest current margins. The SKY Perfect JSAT 5G-NTN win reinforces the space and communications side of that thesis, but it does not materially change the near term catalyst around converting its large defense pipeline into funded production, nor the key risk that heavy upfront CapEx and working capital continue to weigh on free cash flow.

The Airbus Uncrewed Collaborative Combat Aircraft program, built around the Valkyrie, is especially relevant here because it shows Kratos trying to balance capital intensive drone production with more software and systems work like OpenSpace. While Airbus and Valkyrie speak directly to the upside case in unmanned platforms, the SKY Perfect JSAT announcement highlights that OpenSpace and 5G-NTN could become a meaningful second pillar for growth, offering some diversification if large defense awards slip.

Why the DG Matrix partnership matters for Infineon Technologies stock

Infineon Technologies (XTRA:IFX) is in focus after DG Matrix agreed to use its latest-generation silicon carbide MOSFETs in the Interport solid-state transformer platform for AI data centers and electrification projects worldwide.

This agreement links Infineon directly to power infrastructure for AI workloads and utility microgrids, an area investors increasingly watch when assessing how chipmakers are positioned across data center and energy transition themes.

See our latest analysis for Infineon Technologies.

Infineon’s €39.66 share price has seen a 3.51% 1 day share price return but a 13.58% 30 day share price decline, while its 1 year total shareholder return of 19.55% suggests longer term momentum remains stronger than recent trading.

With Infineon trading at €39.66 against an analyst price target of €49.81 and carrying a low value score of 1, you have to ask: is this a mispriced AI power play, or is the market already baking in future growth?

Most Popular Narrative: 21.2% Undervalued

According to the most followed narrative, Infineon’s fair value of €50.32 sits well above the last close at €39.66, setting up a clear valuation gap that investors will want to understand before deciding how this AI power angle fits in their portfolio.

The balance sheet looks very healthy, basically the company is owned by the share holders (equity €17.2b), only a reasonable low share is held by the “banks” (debt €5.4b). Having that in mind, a ROE of 17,5% is a good result, because it would be easy to raise ROE to higher figure, by the leverage effect of debt: IFX could sell more products by increasing the production capacity, that would lead to more result and this would directly increase ROE. The money for more production capacity could be provided from the “banks”, which leads to more debt. That’s the leverage, more debt (at cost x), significant more result (result at 2-5 times x). But IFX operates very moderate, with low debt compared to equity. This leads to a healthy debt to equity ratio of 31,3%.

Approach 1: SOLV Energy Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) Analysis

A Discounted Cash Flow, or DCF, model takes estimates of the cash SOLV Energy may generate in the future and discounts those projections back to today to arrive at an estimated value per share.

For SOLV Energy, the latest twelve month Free Cash Flow is reported at about US$314.6 million. Analyst and extrapolated projections suggest Free Cash Flow of US$92.6 million in 2026, rising to US$448.3 million in 2030, with further years estimated using the same two stage Free Cash Flow to Equity framework. When those projected cash flows are discounted back to today under this model, the resulting intrinsic value is US$37.91 per share. Approach 2: SOLV Energy Price vs Earnings (P/E)

For profitable companies, the P/E ratio is a useful check on value because it links what you pay per share to the earnings that each share generates. It gives you a simple way to see how many dollars investors are currently paying for one dollar of earnings.

A "normal" or "fair" P/E typically reflects what investors expect from a company’s future earnings growth and how much risk they see in those earnings. Higher expected growth or lower perceived risk can support a higher P/E, while slower growth or higher risk usually points to a lower P/E.

SOLV Energy currently trades on a P/E of about 22.79x. That compares with a Construction industry average P/E of around 37.42x and a peer group average of about 26.88x, so the stock is pricing earnings at a lower level than both of those benchmarks.

Simply Wall St’s “Fair Ratio” is a proprietary estimate of what the P/E could be given factors such as SOLV Energy’s earnings growth profile, industry, profit margins, market cap and risk characteristics.
